creator: bourne.identity@hotmail.com
text:
It is often needed by users to test for files and directories in the filesystem:

[ -f <abc> ]
[ -d <xyz> ]

While the tests above work as intended, the tests fail if the entities <abc> / <xyz> are symbolic links. The user often wants to find out:

Is <abc> is a regular file ? Or alternatively, is <abc> a symlink pointing to a regular file ? (Both cases acceptable)

Is <xyz> is a directory ? Or alternatively, is <xyz> a symlink pointing to a directory ? (Both cases acceptable)

The tests above need additional switches, perhaps -F and -D :

-F returns true for regular file as well as valid symlink pointing to regular file.
-D returns true for directory as well as valid symlink pointing to directory.
